steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content: Since both expressions equal y, we set them equal: $$x^2 - 5x + 6 = 6 - x$$

steps.title: Simplify to standard form
steps.content: Move all terms to the left side. Add x to both sides and subtract 6 from both sides: $$x^2 - 5x + x + 6 - 6 = 0$$ $$x^2 - 4x = 0$$

steps.title: Factor and find the solutions
steps.content: Factor out the common factor x: $$x(x - 4) = 0$$ This gives us x = 0 or x = 4. Either value satisfies both equations.
